title: Curry Offal – sticky and delicious

seo.title: Curry Offal - sticky and delicious - Marinda Kook

html:
You see, with offal you mustn't be in a hurry. I don't have the appetite or the desire to clean offal myself. Our Fruit and Veg sold this beautifully cleaned offal. I started cooking it in the pressure pot the previous evening and only finished currying it today.

 

YOU'LL NEED:

  • 1 already cleaned offal
  • 1/2 cup brown grape vinegar
  • juice of 1 lemon
  • 1 can cherry tomatoes or a tomato and onion mixture
  • 40 ml sugar
  • 30 ml vinegar
  • 30 ml curry powder
  • 15 ml turmeric
  • 20 ml cumin (jeera) (you can leave this out if you don't have it)
  • 5 cloves
  • 3 whole cardamom pods
  • 1 bay leaf
  • 10 ml maizena
  • salt and pepper to taste

NOW:

  1. put your offal, 1/2 cup vinegar, juice of 1 lemon and 2 cups water into your pressure pot (if you don't have one, you can do this in an ordinary pot for 2 hours)
  2. now cook it in the pressure pot for 1 hour
  3. in another pot, fry your onions until glassy along with the cumin
  4. scoop the offal out of the pressure pot and fry together with the onions for a bit of colour
  5. mix all the rest of the ingredients together, except the maizena, and pour over the offal in the pot – cook for 2 hours on low heat
  6. after 2 hours, mix the maizena with a bit of water and thicken the sauce of the offal

Delicious to eat with rice or on a slice of bread.
